{
  "reasoning": "The original create_config returned the module DEFAULTS dict itself, not a copy, so callers who modified the returned config would accidentally mutate the module-level defaults. Also, reset_defaults reassigned DEFAULTS to a new dict which could break references to the original dict from other code. I refactored the module to: (1) keep an immutable baseline _BASE_DEFAULTS, (2) expose a mutable DEFAULTS initialized as a copy of the baseline, (3) make create_config return a shallow copy of DEFAULTS before applying overrides to avoid mutating module defaults, and (4) implement reset_defaults to mutate the existing DEFAULTS dict in-place (clear and update from _BASE_DEFAULTS) so references to DEFAULTS remain valid. I also added clearer docstrings and type hints for clarity.",
  "files": {
    "code_snippets_v2/alias_config_a/config.py": "from typing import Dict, Optional

"""Configuration utilities.

Provide a module-level DEFAULTS dict and helpers to create per-call
configurations without accidentally mutating the module defaults.
"""

# Baseline defaults (kept as the authoritative source)
_BASE_DEFAULTS: Dict[str, object] = {"timeout": 30, "retries": 3, "debug": False}

# Exposed module-level defaults (mutable). Initialized from the baseline.
DEFAULTS: Dict[str, object] = _BASE_DEFAULTS.copy()


def create_config(overrides: Optional[Dict[str, object]] = None) -> Dict[str, object]:
    """Return a new config dict based on the module defaults with optional overrides.

    A shallow copy of DEFAULTS is returned so callers can safely modify the
    returned dict without affecting the module-level DEFAULTS.
    """
    config = DEFAULTS.copy()  # return a copy to avoid accidental mutation of DEFAULTS
    if overrides:
        config.update(overrides)
    return config


def reset_defaults() -> None:
    """Reset the module-level DEFAULTS back to the baseline values.

    This mutates the existing DEFAULTS dict in-place (clear + update) so any
    external references to DEFAULTS continue to see the reset values.
    """
    DEFAULTS.clear()
    DEFAULTS.update(_BASE_DEFAULTS)
"
